nl_proof: Explained:
Sentences used:
(1) Penguins do not fly.
(2) John is a penguin.
(3) John flies?
Knowledge used:
If an event X is typical then X is a capability. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
Proof steps (by contradiction):
(1) If answer holds then John performs the event act21  [sentence 3]
(2) Answer holds  [assumption]
(3) John performs the event act21  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If X is a fly event and some penguin W performs X and X is an activity then X is not a capability  [sentence 1]
(5) If answer holds then the event act21 is a fly event  [sentence 3]
(6) The event act21 is a fly event  [from steps 5, 2]
(7) If answer holds then the event act21 is an activity  [sentence 3]
(8) The event act21 is an activity  [from steps 7, 2]
(9) If answer holds then the event act21 is typical  [sentence 3]
(10) The event act21 is typical  [from steps 9, 2]
(11) If an event X is typical then X is a capability  [background knowledge]
(12) The event act21 is a capability  [from steps 10, 11]
(13) John is not a penguin  [from steps 3, 4, 6, 8, 12]
(14) John is a penguin  [sentence 2]
(15) Contradiction  [from steps 13, 14]
